page: Amazon Web Services m8idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. The instance features 4 virtual CPUs derived from 2 physical cores, with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per virtual core.

This instance type includes bundled local storage consisting of a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It is network and EBS optimized, utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor to deliver a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8idn.xlarge balances compute, memory, and high-speed local storage within a single general-purpose profile. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides low-latency disk access, though users must manage the ephemeral nature of instance store volumes. The lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for accelerated computing tasks, positioning it instead for workloads requiring balanced resource distribution.

The resource configuration of the m8idn.xlarge makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, small to medium databases requiring fast local scratch space, web serving, and containerized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel x86_64 processor with 2 physical cores. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance is network and EBS optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. It includes 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage, offering low-latency local disk access. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation provide stable performance for standard computing tasks. Lacking GPU acceleration, the instance is designed for workloads that do not require specialized hardware. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and small database deployments that benefit from fast local ephemeral storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 Intel vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for balanced workloads.

tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel processors, balanced memory, and low-latency local NVMe SSD storage for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel x86_64 processor with 4 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced resource profile
Bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Suitable for web serving, containerized applications, and small databases
